content: WAR OF PROPHECY

PRELUDE

A dozen sinister crimson hooded and red robed figures emit a malicious chant in perfect unison, their voices echoing in the large cavernous chamber. As their incantation reaches a crescendo the world disappears into a flash of harsh blinding green light, all figures gradually  dissolving. At the center of the room, last to dissolve, is a tall imposing figure standing poised to enter the trans-dimensional gateway, glowing green in the center of the cavern, amidst elaborate decorative carvings set in concentric circles into the floor. As the burning green light flares to wipe out all vision, the last visible entity in the room is the wicked yellow-tooth grin on the face of a pale muscular human, partly balding with strands of thin wispy red hair. His features are that of Emperor Palpatine in prime physical condition at middle age, as yet unblemished by Dark Side corruption, with the notable exception of his satanic yellow eyes. An evil cackle emits echoing far into the distance, farther than even the cavern would seemingly allow. You hear: “Son of Skywalker.”

The gleefully malicious cackle redoubles, distorted by some unseen force, and we get the opening crawl…

STAR WARS

WAR OF PROPHECY

*The Galactic Empire reigns supreme. A brave band of rebels, led by Luke Skywalker, have failed in their last ditch attempt to destroy the Second Death Star. Soon, with the superweapon’s completion, the Emperor will hold an unshakable iron grip on the throat of the entire Galaxy.

Blissfully unaware of these momentous events, on the small core world of Typhon, a secluded sect of Jedi, their ancestral line undiminished from the days of the Old Republic, prepare to celebrate the coming of age ceremony for their latest crop of young Jedi Padawans. Finally becoming old enough to hunt and provide for their family, you are one of the most promising new young adults. Given to be raised by the village, once your Force Powers bloomed, you were ripped away from your distant family and brought to the site of the Jedi Academy, having spent your entire life living and studying and breathing in the Essence of The Force, as taught to you by some of the brightest minds of the Praxeum.

However, even in this isolated corner of the galaxy, the fingers of evil reach even the most innocent of souls…*

CHAPTER 1 

CHILD OF PROPHECY


On your final day before graduation, your Grandmaster summons you alone in person. This is the first time for such a meeting, you realize, and begin to get anxious at the possibility of being unable to graduate, although you find that you cannot think of any way you could have displeased the Grandmaster of the Academy.

Grandmaster Ja’hall Gareth stands as you enter: “I’m terribly sorry to tell you this, young Padawan Learner, but you will not be graduating with your class tomorrow.”

Your heart sinks. “May I ask why?”

The Grandmaster stands and faces away from you, “I’ve more important duties for you to attend.”

Your confusion only increases, “More important than tomorrow’s ceremony?”

He suddenly turns to look you square in the eyes, “Much more important.”

You’re left speechless, at which point the Grandmaster softens his harsh tone: “Report at midnight to the Jedi 
 Grand Hall and all shall be revealed. Bring everything of value to you in this life.”

You check your chrono. It is 5 minutes until midnight and the Hall presently stands empty. You use this time to recheck your gear and nervously wonder, what such an ominous command could possibly mean for your future.

For the umpteenth time, Lexi glances at her chrono since arriving in the Grand Hall as bidden by the Grandmaster. She had arrived ten minutes early not wanting to keep the Grandmaster or any of the other Praxeum’s masters waiting on her.

She’d done as she was told and brought everything with her that she owned. Her meager belongings filled up only a small backpack and mostly consisted of a few changes of clothing, the datapad she’d been using for her studies and a blanket one of the villagers had given a few years past on her Star Day. The smaller and more precious items had been secured into the pouches of her utility belt: a woven bracelet, which was now too small for her, but had been crafted by her older and presented just before she had been taken to the academy, and a couple more momentos she’d collected or been given by her friends within the Order. Her self-built lightsaber swings freely from a clip on her belt and her hand goes to it as she performs her mental inventory. She’d restocked the few days of rations the utility belt could hold, not knowing whether the task the Grandmaster had planned for her would involve leaving to parts unknown immediately. *It never hurts to be prepared.*

Looking around once more, the emptiness of the Hall as the hour of midnight approaches increases her unease. Had she misheard when she was supposed to be here? Was she in the right place? She tucks a lock of her blonde hair back behind her ear as she takes a calming breath. Not forgetting her years of training, she opens herself up to the Force, allowing it to fill her with the impressions of her surroundings her other senses can’t provide.

GRAND HALL

At exactly midnight all 12 Jedi Masters, led by Grandmaster Ja’hall, enter in order of seniority. They surround you and form a semicircle with you at the center. “Please follow us,” says Ja’hall and leads the procession back out, after the other Masters have examined you, faint murmurs and surprised gasps can be heard among the dozen.

The senior Jedi remain silent and don’t wait for you to catch up, as they walk single file out of the Grand Hall and lead you into the dense forest, of all things. It is cold at this time of night, and while the Masters seem snuggly warm under their brown Jedi robes, you might be the one left out in the cold dead of night. Predators dare not approach your group, seemingly knowing by instinct not to disturb the apex creatures of this planet, though buzzing insects are another matter.

The Lost Sect of Jedi on Typhon does not possess any interstellar craft, as you know. Or landspeeders for that matter.

Finally after an hour of trekking, mostly up a mountain, you reach a Jedi Temple at the apex, never before seen to you beforehand, and without breaking stride walk up the steps to enter its grounds. The Temple appears abandoned long ago, with piles of rubble and overgrown vines infiltrating most rooms, nevertheless a well worn path appears to lead to the heart of the Temple, whereupon the procession finally halts. All 12 Masters stand in a semicircle to face you once more.

Ja’hall steps forward to break the line and begins to speak, his voice carrying weight and importance:

“Jedi Padawan, Lexi Ardana, step forward.” He ignites his lightsaber, green and verdant. “Kneel.”

If you follow his directions, Ja’hall places his ignited saber over your shoulder, its gentle thrumming heard by one ear, followed shortly by the other. “I now pronounce you, Jedi Knight. You may rise.”

Jedi Master Shan, an old gray-haired crone of a woman, speaks up: “That’s enough theatrics, Ja’hall. We don’t have all damn night, in case you’ve forgotten, the fate of the entire world is at stake. Let’s dispense with this Knights of the Round Table roleplaying shall we.” In response to this new tone, a few chuckles can be heard, as the rest of the Jedi Masters finally relax for the first time after the long, arduous hike. Several take up meditative positions floating in the air, legs crossed, others casually lounge on one of the larger stones littering the Temple. Some are nowhere to be seen. Master Shan, straddling her walking cane, leans against one of the overturned boulders, wiping sweat off her elderly brow. 

Ja’hall furrows his brow initially, at this show of disrespect, but seeing the reaction of his colleagues, simply harrumphs and storms off to another room to leave the Jedi Masters alone with the newly minted Jedi Knight. “Fine, if that’s how you want it, Master Shan, then maybe you should take care of the pleasantries. I’m going to fetch the Prophecy Stones.”

Master Dranella Shan tut-tuts grandmotherly, and pats a stone next to her lap. “Sit down, child. It seems we have a story to tell you, if you’re to finally know the whole truth. Apologies for all the theatrics, Ja’hall can be a bit dense, especially when it comes to important events in the life of a young Jedi Knight. We didn’t intend to disrupt your Knighting ceremony, but it had to be done as soon as possible, legally following the Jedi Code. Now that you’ve been properly initiated into the fold, you have the right to know everything we know. Unlike the Sith, there are no secrets among a family of Jedi.” A raspy cough emits.

“Let’s begin at the beginning. A dozen years ago, Jedi archaeologists unearthed a series of stone tablets, which when translated from Old Republic, describe the Prophecy of a unique Jedi. One who will bring balance to the Force and help to lift it from darkness. The name of this Jedi on the tablets is unknown to us: An’akin. We searched far and wide among every corner of our globe in secret but no one by that name was discovered to be on Typhon. From our ancient computer database, no such name carries any history of note, meaning the Prophecy has yet to be fulfilled. We didn’t think much of these developments, until yesterday - out of the blue all the Jedi Masters felt a great disturbance in the Force. I’m sure you felt it too. We consulted all of our scholars, and to our shock the name An’akin had disappeared from the Prophecy Stones, instead replaced by a different name entirely, yours: “Ar’dana.” The spelling leaves no doubt as to whom is to be the next Child of Prophecy, and given that you’re the only Jedi from your Clan, it leaves you as the undisputed savior of the entire Galaxy. Sorry to break it to you, kiddo, but it looks like you’re our best hope. For whatever it is, that The Force Wills.” She settles her chin somberly onto her cane, coughing repeatedly.

Striding into the room confidently and with full force, Grandmaster Ja’hall returns to the central chamber, a series of heavy looking stone tablets levitating in his wake. He nods to Shan, indicating the name on the tablets has not changed. Alongside the powerfully built Grandmaster, a four legged alien canine predator follows. The new creature has white fur and three eyes, along with the muscled powerful body of a dire wolf. It speaks telepathically to everyone in the room, its calm feminine voice echoing inside your mind. “Time is short, Two Legs. The Spirit Moon is obscured by clouds, but once the clouds part, we shall begin.”

One of the younger Jedi Masters jumps onto his feet and laughs playfully, “Woa, so that’s Lathelia, who’s a good doggy!” As he goes to pet the new canine, it parts its lips to reveal a massive maw filled with sharpened dagger-size teeth. “Okay okay just saying hello!”

At the apex of the chamber, in the roof, you notice a circular opening through which the partly revealed blue-green moon of Typhon can be seen. The cyan sphere gives off a radiant glow this night, although at this exact moment, its lower half is covered by thick dark clouds. 

Master Shan coughs her dusty cough, as if trying to get the very last bit of exposition off her chest before she croaks. 

“We’ve all gathered tonight, to perform a ritual to transport you off Typhon. You’ll be whisked away to the Realm of the Force, what we call The World Between Worlds. Within, you’ll have to heed the Will of the Force, it will guide you where to go. Who knows what time or place you’ll end up in, just be mindful and allow the Force to guide your actions. Be careful, if the newly written Prophecy is correct, then you don’t have much time to change the fate of the Galaxy. Your opponent will be a mysterious Sith Lord, who also appears to have such powers as ours. Now, prepare yourself.” A sudden coughing fit seizes the old Master. After Master Shan catches her breath, the moon suddenly becomes fully visible and lights up the Jedi Temple in its verdant glow.

“Enough horseplay.” Announces Ja’hall. “Get into position and prepare to perform your duties. Break time is over. Gather your power, 70% from each and every one of you. 80% from you.” He points at the young master who attempted to pet the Loth wolf.

Responding with variably toned affirmatives, the tired Jedi Masters gradually stand up and begin to form a wide circle about the central Temple Chamber, stretching and groaning.

You’re left alone in the very center of the room, the cyan glow of the moon bathing you in twinkling moonlight. 

Presently, not everyone has as yet taken up formation, the Jedi Masters ending their levitations and limbering up, groaning and yawning, gathering their strength, in preparation for some kind of Force ritual. You feel as though this might be your last chance to converse, before whatever it is they’ve planned is set to go off.

Additionally, you can interact with any of the Jedi Masters you wish, if you want to create your own personal Jedi Master here and fill in their backstory of training you, you should feel free to do so in great detail, as it may effect the rest of your adventure immensely, or not at all. Perhaps you have developed romantic feelings for one of the younger Masters, again this may later become extremely important, or not important whatsoever.

Lexi stops herself from sighing in relief when the dozen Praxeum masters finally arrive as her chrono ticks to midnight. Their appearance at last easing her concerns that she had somehow mistaken Grandmaster Ja'hall's instructions. The feeling is short lived as a pit forms in her stomach as they encircle her, placing her in the center of attention. Uneasy under their scrutiny, she shifts from one foot to the other. 

The Padawan glances between each in turn as they murmur among themselves before filing out behind Master Ja'hall. As the last master begins to leave her behind she draws in a deep breath, letting it out in a rush before she sets off after them. 

When it becomes apparent they were leaving the academy grounds into the cold night air, she draws her cloak closer against her, glad for its warmth however meager it might be. Having spent the majority of her life within the confines of the Academy and the nearby village, their trek through the woods and up the mountain comes as a surprise. She’d never come this way and her head turns one way and then the other as she takes in what she can in the dark of night. Through the Force she can sense the life around them as the local fauna make note of their passing. Her eyes grow wide as she gets her first glimpse of the mountain temple. 

Lexi had been looking intently at one of the derelict arches as she passed, but then comes up short as she realizes the master in front of her is taking his place in the same semi-circular formation from before. Now once again the center of their attention, she takes in a calming breath to center her nerves. The strange excursion and the presence of all twelve of the Praxeum's masters left nothing but questions in her thoughts, but she has the presence of mind to hold her tongue. The grandmaster had told her everything would be revealed, and she trusted the master would hold true to his word.

When she's called forth to kneel before Ja'hall, his lighstaber held at the ready and ignited at his side, she hesitates only a moment before she complies. Self consciously, she tucks the stray hair back behind her ear and releases her breath as she closes the distance. Kneeling before him, she bows her head in supplication. When the thrum of the lightsaber comes to hover over her shoulder she dares not move as he completes the Knighting ceremony. Her head comes up with his saber and she looks at him with mild surprise.  

When he had told her she wouldn't be joining the rest of her friends for the Ceremony she had been disappointed, thinking she had done something after all to warrant not advancing within the ranks of the Order. That she was perhaps being sent away to fulfill different duties that didn't need to be performed by a Jedi Knight. Now though, even without the pomp and ceremony that would be occurring later in the day, she was a Knight. Yet, the purpose of this enterprise was yet to be revealed as promised.

Standing, she takes a step back. There's relief from the other masters as the ever vocal Master Shan speaks and they all disperse to their own pursuits. If it wasn't for the elderly Master’s words, Lexi too may have found some relief. *Fate of the entire world is at stake?* The pit begins to form in her stomach again and she forces it away as Master Shan bids her to take a seat beside her. 

Lexi listens attentively to the tale of prophecy, but as the aged master reaches the end she has to forcefully close her mouth. The shock of the revelation turns to disbelief. “**Me?!**” she utters, but anything else she was about to say is encompassed by the emergence of the Grandmaster, the accursed tablets naming her as the galaxy's savior trailing behind on currents of the Force. No, not her specifically, her clan. Prophecy was always rife with misinterpretation, perhaps there was still a chance to sort this out.

She misses the arrival of the giant wolf and the young Jedi has to do a double take as it communicates with them telepathically, the feminine tones intruding on her raging thoughts. More questions arise as Master Shan explains the scope of the mission she was about to be sent on. Alone. To a dimension where she'd only have the Force to guide her. She takes in slow steady breaths to keep herself from hyperventilating and find a calm center, her training kicking in. Gazing up at the cascading, cyan moonlight cast down on her from the roof's opening, she takes a drawn out breath before releasing it.

There’s a determined set to her shoulders as Lexi takes the opportunity the sudden break the masters’ preparation affords her and she goes in search of her one true ally among the masters. Master Davian Hux, one of the youngest and newly established as one of the distinguished Praxeum masters had found himself a secluded corner of the temple. He couldn’t hide from her; his presence in the Force drawing her like a moth to a flame. 

All sense of decorum is out the window as Lexi seeks the comfort of his embrace. “**I don't understand. None of this makes sense.**” Her words tumble out, all the doubt and questions surging to the surface in an indecipherable torrent. “**I've never even heard of this World Between Worlds. You're sending me away and I haven't heard anything about how I'm supposed to get back… If I'll be coming back. And a Sith Lord.**” She draws in a breath before panic begins to set in. “**I’m supposed to be going up against a Sith Lord… alone?!**” Lexi's fists cling to the front of his robe as she draws back to look up at him. Davian’s pale skin glows blue in the moonlight and his usual jovial expression grim. He runs his fingers through his short cropped brown hair, leaving the sections he touches sticking up. It was a sign he felt as helpless as her in what was to come.

Davian hugs Lexi in his arms, kissing her on the top of her head, emotion evident in the quivering of his tone. **“Hush Lexi, you’re ready for this, you’ve been training for something like this your whole life. I know this is all very sudden and unexpected, but you’re literally the Chosen One. There’s no one else who can take your place. I promise that wherever the Force takes you, I’ll be with you in spirit. At the very least, try to remember my teachings. It’s the only way I can think of to help you.”** He tears up a bit too, at the prospect of losing her so quickly, her traveling through time and space, potentially never to return.

"**Chosen One?!**" Lexi nearly hisses incredulously. "**All because some tablet with a prophecy names my clan.  It was someone else before and now it's me.**" Her iron grip on his cloak loosens as she draws in a breath to regain control.

**"I know... take this with you..."** He removes a plain woven necklace, at the end of it dangles a khyber crystal. Fingering the charm in his hand, he covers her fingers around it. **"Take this, and may the Force be with You."**

The Masters seem to observe their private moment, as all eyes are upon the new Jedi Knight, you feel their gaze staring daggers at your back, despite trying to hide in the arms of Davian. Was this even fair to ask of someone? To sacrifice their whole life on a dusty prophecy?

Taking the necklace in hand by the crystal, she gazes into it for a moment before placing the cord over her head. It was then she felt the eyes of the other masters on them. She found, suddenly, she didn't care. They had spent their short years together being as discreet as a pair of young people could be in their close knit community. Now, they may never see each other again.
Drawing closer, she pulls up on the balls of her feet to close the distance between them. Her fingers grasp the back of his head for one final kiss. She didn't want to say goodbye.  There was something too final about a farewell. Falling back on her heels she gazes up at him, only to remember everything about this moment. "**Thank you Davian... I won't forget.**"

**"You don't have to go, you know."** A new Jedi Master's voice cuts sharply into your conversation. You recognize it as the tenor and tone of Davian's rival, Master Norv Thrantir, **"I never believed in that prophecy mumbo jumbo myself, just a bunch of nerds digging through old trash, if you ask me. I've already spoken with the Grandmaster and I told him you'd chicken out. Just give the word, and I'll go through the portal in your stead. I intend to definitively prove to everyone that I am the true Chosen One."** Hefting a massive saberstaff over one shoulder and wearing gold leather gloves, Master Thrantir smirks as he locks eyes with Davian.

Lexi turns on her heel away from Davian as Thrantir delivers his proposition. His plan had more merit then sending a newly initiated Jedi Knight to face as of yet unknown perils. Yet something about it felt wrong. 
"**I'll abide by the Masters' wisdom... If it's me that must go, then I will.**"
Casting a final look back at Davian, she heads back towards the center of the room.

Taking the rejection seemingly in stride to save face, Thrantir proceeds to rant to no one in particular, **"Oh well... Seems like a waste of perfectly good talent, if you ask me."** He looks you up and down. **"Are we really sending little girls now to handle the heavy lifting? Does anyone really think she can match a Sith Lord? But of course, as usual my opinion gets ignored by everyone else on this counsel. For the last time, we should not be relying upon etchings made into abandoned rocks as driving our Order's entire foreign policy."** Thrantir has long been threatening to leave the Jedi Order, over multiple differences in philosophy. This time however, the Grandmaster cuts him short: **"That'll be all Thrantir, you've made your objections perfectly clear. Now get in formation and enter meditative stance. That goes for the rest of you as well."**

It's with effort, the young Knight allows Thrantir’s words to wash over her. Davian was right in that she had spent her life training, maybe not for this specific situation, to be able handle herself when the time came. The shock had been that she was now being cast out to handle the unknown on her own without having had the time to acclimate to her new role within the Order. 

Lexi draws her cloak closer around herself while she watches for a moment the other masters beginning to gather. She tries not to let her gaze linger on Davian as he takes his position and gives her a reassuring smile before his focus draws inward. With questions still left unanswered, she approaches Master Ja’hall as he organizes the council into place for the ritual. Everyone else seemed to know what was expected of them, and what to expect. Other than the knowledge she was going to be whisked away to the World Between Worlds she didn't know how that was to occur or what she might expect when she arrived there. The only guidance she had was to follow the Will of the Force. 

“**Master,**” she starts in an attempt to get the grandmaster's attention. “**This ritual: what exactly does it entail? Master Shan says I'll be sent to a World Between Worlds, but I've never heard of such a place.**”

Grandmaster Gareth turns and looks down in your direction: **"The ritual will allow you to leave Typhon, that is all you need know. Leave the details to us, trust me when I say you should focus on your own part and let us worry about doing ours. I'm sorry there just isn't enough time to brief you on every detail. Let me at least give you a general rundown, however, of what we do know. This ritual has not been performed in thousands of years. What we know of the Force Dimension, we only know about through ancient Jedi texts. From what they tell us, you will enter a strange World that sits apart from our reality, made of pathways and doors to very important Nexuses in the History of Time and Space. The dimension will only allow you to see what is relevant to your personal journey, so your thoughts should be unclouded to allow you to find and eventually enter the correct gateway - the one to hopefully lead you to saving our Galaxy. I know it seems abstract, but if you allow the Force to guide your actions, you will be following the true path. Trust your intuition. Once there, you will be able to access places and events both from the past and from the future. We don't know the exact nature of the threat, apart from the fact that our ancient enemy - the Sith - are once again at the helm of the latest wicked scheme to destroy us. Our hopes all rest on your shoulders, young Jedi Knight. May the Force be With You."**

Lexi listens intently to whatever information Master Ja’hall can give her regarding what she was about to face. It wasn't much, and was all she was going to get in the short time afforded to her. 

The enormity of the task once again sends her mind reeling and she has to force down her increasing trepidation. Instead she nods, understanding from his tone she had been dismissed and he had spared what time he would answering her questions. “**Thank you, Master.**” Still unsure of her role in the coming ritual, she steps back waiting for further instruction. 

To ease her growing nerves, she closes her eyes and draws in the Force around her. The young Knight allows it to flow through her in an effort to find her center.

Jedi Master Xoda gently glides up to you, legs crossed, holding your green lightsaber handle in both hands reverently. Master Xoda belongs to a diminutive race of alien beings known to be exceedingly long-lived, but also nearly extinct in the galaxy. The name of the far flung race is unknown to this day, although every member has been a massive conduit of Force potential. **“My task as Jedi Master Engineer is to provide you with all the tools the Hero of the Order could ever require. Your lightsaber has been upgraded to last longer on a single charge, and I have personally installed a full suite of modern tech modifications, from magnetic grip compatible with your glove, to the included onboard computer to store all the information you gather during your quest, and finally our most state of the art telecommunication array with holorecorder and holographic projector. Your green khyber crystal has also been encased in a protective shell casing. You must not let your khyber crystal come to harm, because it represents your soul’s life force. Our engineers worked tirelessly to craft this weapon to be weatherproof, waterproof, and generally foolproof. Heheh.”** The serious facade cracks and Master Xoda laughs in an inappropriate silly manner. **“Try not to look so glum, little tadpole. You’re going on an adventure of a lifetime! You could look at it as a dreadful experience, but as my Grandmaster Xaga used to say: ‘You color your experience of the world in the manner of your choosing.’ Many of us ancient Jedi Masters would give up everything to be able to go wherever it is… you’re destined to end up. The Force has chosen you, little tadpole. That means great things are in store. Great things indeed, mhm.”** He starts giggling to himself, **“Mrhm hrm heehee... ”**and generally goes off on his own to make goofy noises to himself, snorting and laughing in an inappropriate manner on his own, as if laughing to himself at indecipherable internal humorous anecdotes.

Lexi takes her saber from the engineer, turning over the hilt in her hand, surprised to hear of the modifications which were now housed within the small form. “**Thank you, Master Xoda,**” she says, giving the floating Jedi master a small bow. 

As she listens to the rest of his advice, she clips the lightsaber to her belt. She got the feeling that while this evening had been short notice for her, the other masters had had more time to prepare. 

When was the disturbance they had felt? For a long moment she tried to think back. While the masters had seemed unnerved that day, there hadn't been anything else to mark the day as different than any others. 

Her attention comes back to Master Xoda as he begins to move away, extolling how fortunate she was to be going on such an adventure. “**I'll keep that in mind,**” she says slowly, her tone level to hide her own displeasure.

Next to approach is Thrantir. *"Master Xoda worked tirelessly all night with his team of engineers to upgrade your saber. Guard your weapon close, that weapon is your life.... Well, that's what I'm supposed to tell you anyway. As for my real advice--"* Thrantir produces a tiny-sized holdout blaster pistol from the fold of his robes and places it into your possession in a fluid efficient manner that suggests he's comfortable handling such weapons, *"I figured you could use something more... practical."* 

He unholsters his own ornate gold-embroidered Blaster Pistol, whips it around in a lightning fast orbit and reholsters it with one golden leather glove. *"Sorry kid, this one's mine but I figured you could use something handy in case you actually come face to face with a Sith Lord. These Jedi nerds don't think like us regular folk, they think they're so high and mighty and above using blasters. Tsk tsk, it's a sad sight that our Order has been reduced to brainwashed dimwits and I'm the sole spark of intellect in this whole herd of sheep. Let me break it down to you. This is called a Holdout Blaster, it's specially made to fit into a discreet area, like in the small of your back, or between those well endowed pillows you're smuggling. A Holdout Blaster doesn't pack the most whopper of a punch, but it'll actually do the job that you need to do when you need it done. Yeah right now you're thinking I'm all sorts of rude, but trust me when you're backed into a tight corner, you'll be thinking of this conversation in an altogether different light. I don't know what you see in that vanilla-flavored loser Davian, but if you do ever come back alive from this ordeal, maybe this weapon will change your mind about who the real hotshot is around here."*

Davian has stepped in between you and Thrantir, interposing himself into your conversation. Master Thrantir changes cadence to sound more regal, *"... and in conclusion, May the Force be with you, young Jedi Knight."* Bowing gracefully in what you recognize to be a mocking manner, Thrantir leaves the Holdout Blaster in your hands, and proceeds to make his exit. 

By now everyone has gathered in their places, save for Davian whose eyes stare longingly and deeply into yours.*"Goodbyes are hard for me…"* He seems more or less at a loss for words. The kiss you gave him earlier awakened deeper feelings within, instead of quenching them you’ve only spurred him on further, it seems. He tucks your chin upward, looking deeply into your face and tilting his own face to match yours. *“Every Chosen One in Prophecy has their life partner, I’m sorry I won’t be there to fulfill that role. I sense we would have been perfect for one another. I hope you return one day, finding your way back won’t be easy. Over the last 24 hours I’ve scoured and collected everything regarding the location of ancient Jedi Temples around the known Galaxy. Seek them out when you’re ready to come back to me… I’ll be waiting.”* Gifting her an ancient Jedi Tome, Davian bends down for one last kiss before you can even respond, his lips hungrily devouring yours as he moans in pleasure.

Unfortunately after just a few seconds in, Grandmaster Ja’hall Gareth backhands Davian in the face and sweeps his arm across with such force, as to force the younger Jedi Master to stumble away from your clutches. Gareth seems angry but also in control: *“Such behavior is unbecoming of a Jedi, irregardless the hardships our Order endures. The only reason you still breathe is because you weren’t stupid enough to take away a Padawan’s virginity, but don’t mistake my mercy for any kind of approval. You should have never gotten this close with one of your students.”* 

Davian calmly bows his head to the rest of the Masters seemingly ignoring Ja’hall’s outburst, *“Masters, I would never take advantage of my position as a teacher to infatuate myself with any of my charges. I think everyone present here saw that this young lady came into my embrace of her own volition, sought my affection first, and being a Jedi Knight entitles her to kiss whomever she desires. I think we can all agree the Code is clear on that point.”* 

Davian seems entirely emotionless and turns back to Gareth, “Grand Master I apologize if my conduct seemed to approach a line we Jedi have sworn never to cross. I merely wished to use my position as this Knight’s new lover to comfort her in the most trying moment of her entire life. I think we would all agree she could use every boost to morale we can muster. Grand Master I will also add, that your conduct doesn’t reflect the leadership befitting of a Grandmaster of this Order, in my humble opinion.”* The two males stare at each other with stern expressions. Harsh whispers break out among the Jedi Masters.

After about a minute, Master Dranella Shan strides forward to interpose herself between Gareth and Davian. *“Boys boys boys, you just can’t help yourselves but get violently overprotective when a young maiden provides you the opportunity to do so, it seems. Master Davian is right, and Ja’hall you certainly owe the young couple an apology. At the same time, Master Gareth is also right, we saw how you singled out this Padawan out for special treatment Davian, don’t try to use your wily words to escape what were surely less than pure intentions on your part. You think I wasn’t a desirable maiden once? For your male idiocy, you’ll both be doing rock lifts until daybreak until your overdeveloped arms fall off. As the oldest female in the Order, I have to come down here and fix your testosterone fueled escapades and I don’t appreciate them one bit. Let’s all agree that everyone has apologized, made up, and get this damn ritual over and done while the Spirit Moon is at its zenith.”* She arches her brows at the young couple: *“Say your goodbyes in solitude, that’s the best I can provide, given the circumstances...”* She drags Ja’hall away by his ear, continuing her rant offscreen.

The tiny blaster felt odd in her hand when Thrantir placed it there. While her weapons training had focused on the use of the lightsaber there had been some instruction with blasters. The gift itself was a surprise, and she's about to thank him for it when Davian interjects between them. As Thrantir turns to leave she gives him a nod of thanks and a less mocking bow to show her appreciation for the blaster and his advice. She wasn't about to turn down any sort of help at this point, regardless of where it came from.

Lexi's focus turns strictly to Davian as he steps in closer to her, their usual restraint while in the presence of others clearly forgone. “**We won't say goodbye then,**” she starts, but her words are cut short as he takes hold of her chin.  

She didn't know how the ancient temple sites were involved in traveling between the dimensions, but she was grateful nonetheless. It provided some hope that one day she may be able to return; Force willing. Lexi nearly drops the gifted tome as catches her lips with hers, the urgent need taking her breath away. 

When he’s suddenly pulled away, she gives a grunt in surprise and her face grows warm at the sudden embarrassment. All discretion forgotten, Davian was now getting the brunt of the criticism. She's thankful for Master Shan as she intervenes in the debacle leaving her and Davian alone once more.

Looking around, there wasn't going to be any solitude to be found in the midst of the masters and their preparations for the ritual. Not looking at Davian, she draws her backpack from her shoulders and places the tome within, not having anywhere else on her person large enough to hold it. Checking the safety mechanism, she puts the holdout blaster into one of the large pockets of her cloak.

Finally collected, she stands to face him. She takes his hand allowing their fingers to interlace. “**We won't say goodbye,**” she reiterates. “**I don't know when I'll return, but I'll find a way.**” Holding him at bay from kissing her once more she instead takes the moment to inscribe him to her memory. If he kissed her again, she wasn't sure she would be able to keep her resolve to leave.

Davian smiles sadly, “I know.” He grasps her shoulders and kisses her for the third and last time. After a long minute of whipping his neck around desperately and nearly inhaling your breath from your lungs, he releases you and turns away, before you can say another word. You catch the brief sight of a single tear threatening to build up inside his eyelid as he turns and closes his eyes to face away in sorrow, walking away without ultimately tearing up, though obviously trembling with great emotion. Davian passes by and nearly shoulder barges through Ja’hall as the Grandmaster walks up to give Lexi her last instructions:

“Go now, young one, with the bidding of the Force. Take your place in the center and as soon as I open the portal, go through to the World Between Worlds.” He pauses, waiting to catch your gaze, as he smiles gently. “You two make a nice coupling, just don’t forget attachment leads to the Dark Side. If you cannot hold temptation at bay by sheer force of will, you stand no chance against the Sith Lord. You symbolize the Jedi Order itself: a perfect paragon sacrificing everything to save the entire galaxy. Wherever you walk, always remember, you represent not only yourself but also the entire idea of the Jedi themselves. Always be honorable in all your actions, nothing less than that. Never hesitate, if it feels right, do it, without delay. The Force works through you, I believe in you. Never doubt yourself. Believe in yourself unashamedly.”

Grandmaster Ja’hall Gareth clasps his hands together, closing his eyes in deep concentration, as we fade away. We observe each of the Jedi Masters in turns closing their eyes in concentration, taking meditative positions at exactly 12 points around the circular room, like the markings on a clock face. Davian closes his eyes last, and a single teardrop rolls down from the corner of one eye.

The 3-eyed telepathic dire wolf stalks inside the circle of Masters, approaching you for the first time. Next, Lothelia makes a circle around you, muscles beneath its thick fur coat wobbling with every step, its motion slow and dream-like in the sparkling motes of light that float from the center of the ceiling. The light motes settle into the fur of the Loth Wolf and onto your clothes, illuminating and giving you both an unearthly glow. The Loth Wolf sits down on its haunches and raises its massive throat to the sky towards the Spirit Moon, emitting an alien howl that echoes against the surrounding mountains endlessly. As soon as the wolf does so, the very fabric of space in front of Lexi begins to warp, wobble and tear open in a display of heat haze. Multiple streams of multicolor energy flow away from every Jedi Master connecting with the interdimensional cyan portal gradually expanding at the center of the chamber. The runes built into the floor of the Jedi Temple begin to glow a cyan glow, thrumming with power. You notice the floor separately illuminates three obvious stone-carved paw prints, glowing yellow. A strange vibration is palpable in the air, vibration waves synchronizing with drafts of the oxidized smell of ozone.

As the portal grows ever larger, a wind picks up in the room, causing capes and robes to buffet about wildly, flapping in the gale. At last, the portal is large enough to permit entry. The Loth wolf digs in behind you, protecting you from being swept away. You feel its warm alien fur against your back. You reach one hand out to the portal, in anticipation, fighting the gust of wind emanating from within. You hear the spirit wolf’s telepathic voice, emotionless and feminine, “Wait for a break in the Force winds, as soon as the wind falters - take your chance to step through.” You’re thankful that you received those instructions clearly, because the deafening sounds of the windstorm presently prevent you from hearing entirely. You wait patiently, and just when you feel an eternity passes, the winds abruptly shift and nearly pull you towards the portal instead of pushing you away. You fail to take your chance, hesitating from fear, and freeze in place. Suddenly, a gust of Force power pushes you the final distance all the way into the portal. The world turns into a spinning twister, as the horizon rotates around you like inside a washing machine. Bright cyan lights engulf your vision and you lose consciousness, unbeknownst to you, the cyan color has permanently replaced the color of your green irises, but not before you’re treated to a terrible nightmarish sight.

As you get Force pushed into the portal, time slows, Typhon recedes away from you, as you float weightlessly. In slow motion you watch the scene of the Jedi Temple shrink, when suddenly you realize a new presence inside the temple has made itself known. A squad of black-robed figures adorned with Dark Armor plates, wielding red lightsabers, metallic masks hiding their faces, enters the Jedi Temple and catches the Jedi Masters off guard. The gang of Sith fighters can now be observed in ultra slow motion from your perspective, simultaneously slaying several Masters outright whilst crossing blades with Davian, Thrantir, and Ja’hall amongst others. Jedi Master Shan has taken a lethal red blade through her torso but maintains eye contact. Her lips mouth, “Go~” as blood runs down her chin, and Master Shan holds both palms forward in your direction, having Force thrust you the final way through the portal, after which the world tips over and you disappear, traveling into the interdimensional space between worlds. The background turns very light blue, the frozen scene of the ritual chamber crystallizing into light blue crystals of energy that rapidly shrink and disappear, taking the rest of the world away. Pitch blackness is all that remains.

Lexi's hands grip Davian's robes as he entraps her in a final, desperate kiss. The ferocity leaves her lips numb and her chest heaving as he reluctantly steps away. She shuts her eyes tight against her own tears as he walks away from her, passing by the grandmaster. Not able to stand the sight of Davian walking away from her, she turns away. It was taking all her strength and resolve in the importance of the task set before her to not just leave it all behind.

At first Ja'hall’s words pass over her, then he pauses expectantly until she at last turns her attention to the grandmaster. She half listens to his lecture, her thoughts wandering elsewhere. His message, which worded differently, was one she'd heard countless times over the years of her training. 

First a gentle chastisement regarding the dangers of attachment. While the masters turned a seemingly blind eye to the relationships developed by members of the Order, she knew they were quick to interfere. Then he moved onto reminders of comportment and above all: trusting in the Force.    Lexi nods studiously and lets out a sigh when Ji'hall moves away to finalize his own preperations. 

Securing her bag to her back once more, she heads over to the tablets which proclaimed the identity of Chosen One. Proclaimed her to be the Chosen One following a disturbance in the Force which had transferred it to her. Something had happened within the last few days which had set this series of events in motion. Not daring to touch the sacred stones, she gets as close as she's able. 

The strange direwolf hovered nearby. *Was she somehow tied to the stones or the World Between Worlds?* Lexi wonders. “**You're called Lothelia?**” she starts, watching the strange direwolf with curiosity. “**Will you be acting as my guide?**” she dares to ask the large wolf, remembering it had spoken telepathically to them all earlier.

**"You shall find others of my kind to guide you along your path, Two Legs. Remember that my race is attuned to other beings with Force Sensitivity. If you progress along the path your Masters refer to as Animal Empathy, you will be able to attract others of my species, if they exist out there somewhere in the wilderness of time and space. My sole job here is to complete the Ritual, the rest is up to you - you will be on your own I'm afraid."** The dire wolf licks you reassuringly.
PART 2 WORLD BETWEEN WORLDS

You materialize in the void of space, yet somehow you’re not dead from freezing or lacking oxygen. The twinkling stars of the farflung void stretch out in every direction, punctuated at rare intervals by gates interconnected by long winding celestial pathways, in an Escher-like manner. You stand looking in every direction, observing that you’re standing on a single pathway.

As you progress along your celestial walkway, there only seems to be one path before it branches, you peer into the portals along the way in sequence. It appears each of the portals hold an extended vision of a critical event in the Galaxy. One after another, you peer into 7 large portals as you walk along a single continuous path [These correspond to the 6 Lucas Movie Films and Rogue One Film, allowing your character to learn the events of each of those movies, as if having watched them. However, there has been a change to Episode 6. At the end of Episode 6, instead of being defeated, Palpatine triumphs over Luke Skywalker, with the aid of a mysterious new Sith that appears in the Throne Room Scene. The new Sith convinces Palpatine that he is his own Force clone from the future, and warns Emperor Palpatine that Vader together with Skywalker will betray and kill Palpatine. The Sith Lords and Royal Guards prepare an ambush. As a result, Vader along with Luke Skywalker are both taken by surprise and eventually succumb to their wounds, leaving Palpatine alive with the younger version of himself to gloat in their victory. Episode 6 ends with the future clone of Palpatine killing old Emperor Palpatine, in order to make certain his existence comes to pass]. The time you spend appears to go by quickly, as you absorb all this information from each portal in a visual manner, as well as via Force telepathy, though you’re not exactly sure how that last part functions.

After passing 7 gateways, you finally come to a fork in the road. The single path you’ve been walking splits. Ahead stand four pathways to 4 different portals. Approaching and inspecting each of the portals reveals a different Era of the Star Wars Galaxy.

Portal 1. This Portal depicts the events of the first two books of the Bane Trilogy, culminating in the destruction of the Brotherhood of Darkness and the formation of the Rule of Two. Entering this portal transports your character backwards to the year 1000 BBY to experience the events of the Bane Trilogy, starting with the Academy Training Arc. Your character opts to join the Sith Academy, where you get a chance to train alongside the young versions of Darth Bane and Darth Zannah. You will travel to the same planets Darth Bane goes to, if you earn his trust.

Portal 2. This Portal depicts the events of the Separatist Crisis showing the rise of the Trade Federation, Darth Maul, Senator Palpatine, and his election to Chancellor. Entering this portal transports your character backwards into 32 BBY to experience the events of Episode 1, though in this alternate version of events instead of meeting Jar Jar Binks, Qui-gon and Obi-wan run into you instead. The character of Jar Jar doesn’t exist and is overwritten by yours.

Portal 3. This Portal depicts the events of the Clone Wars culminating in Order 66, with the rise of Darth Vader, the Galactic Empire, murder of most of the Jedi Order, and Palpatine’s destruction of the Republic. Entering this portal transports your character backwards into 19 BBY to experience the events of Episode 3.

Portal 4. This Portal depicts the events of the Thrawn Trilogy, culminating in the discovery of the secret storehouse on Mount Tantis, except that in this version of event Joras C’baoth does not exist and in his place is a young clone of Emperor Palpatine, who teams up with Thrawn. Entering this portal transports your character forward to 9 ABY to experience the events of the Alternate Thrawn Trilogy.

Normally I try to remain secretive and spoiler-free, but in this case the next decision will effect so much of the game, I felt you have the right to know the estimated result of your decision. I haven't finalized all the Timeline Changes, so nothing is set in stone, but those are roughly the 4 choices available. Selecting Portal 1 means you'll be attempting to time-erase the formation of the Rule of Two. Without the Rule of Two, Palpatine's predecesors do not come to be and neither does he. Selecting Portals 2 or 3 means you'll be attempting to time-erase the man Palpatine himself, before he is able to spring his scheme upon the Republic. Selecting Portal 4 means you'll be trying to time-erase Clone Palpatine before he can time-erase Luke Skywalker. Those are essentially what the choices boil down to.

The blackness enfolds Lexi in its embrace as she enters the portal. No. She was pushed in. When the way had become clear she had hesitated. She had allowed her fear of the unknown to get the better of her. 

As she comes to in the void, images of the events at the temple run through her mind. The horror of the events that had transpired as she debarted into this realm leaving her hollow inside. Questions and concerns rise to the surface followed by a frustration with the knowledge she may never know the fate of those she leaves behind.  

Time, she quickly realizes, has no meaning in this realm. Past and future exist as one and accessible through the various portals she finds along the path. Visions of events play over the surface of the portals along her path and she stops at each. She learns the history of the galaxy as it exists outside of the isolation of Typhon and of the path the previous Chosen One. No wonder the masters had been unable to find him. Anakin Skywalker was born to the wider galaxy where he had been able to make a difference. Though as he falls to the machinations of the Sith Lord, she ultimately wonders if the Chosen One has ultimately failed as the balance shifts to the Dark Side as untold millions died under his dominion.

Then Lexi spots where things must have changed as a younger, more knowledgeable Palpatine surfaces. Anakin turned Darth Vader is ambushed and killed along with his son and the Dark Side prevails. She stands before the last gate for a long while, hoping it would go back to where everything went wrong. It grows dim and her chance to fix the mistake, to set things back on track as it was meant to be disappears.

With no other options, she resumes her journey along the celestial pathway. The path behind her disappears when she finds herself in the center of a crossroads. Her way back is replaced with only a path forward.

Lexi turns in a slow circle as the options play out, repeating on themselves. One she realizes is from the far distant past when the Sith were consolidating their power and moving to the shadows. Two more were more recent and would allow her to be involved in the events surrounding the rise of Palpatine's power. The last must be the future with Palpatine putting his dark plans to action without Anakin or his son to counter him. The fate of the Chosen One's secret daughter had not been revealed to her.

These were the choices the Force was providing her to fulfill her destiny. Lexi closes her eyes and takes in a deep calming breath before letting it out slowly. The decision before her hangs heavy, but the Force was leaving it to her to decide the path. She could change the past, or affect the future. Her eyes open and her gaze falls on the gate of the future. Too much could go wrong if she went into the past and Anakin turned Vader had had his chance to fulfill his destiny. To set things right, she would have to deal with the fallout.

Lexi steps towards the gate of the future, the celestial pathway fading behind her as her intentions become clear. Now in front of the gate she looks closely at it, taking in the various symbols wondering their meaning. Absently, she draws her lightsaber and uses the holorecorder built within to take a snapshot.

Releasing a breath she had been holding, she reaches out her hand to the portal before stepping through.

INTRODUCTION

The year 9 ABY.

The Galaxy has been held in the iron grip of the Galactic Empire by Emperor Palpatine, who has deflected countless attempts to overthrow his sovereignty. Thanks to his ISB, he has managed to thwart any rebellion from within, but now a new threat to the stability of his glorious Empire emerges, as rumors have it, from the fringes of the Galaxy.

The Unknown Regions have long been a haven for those wishing to escape galactic civilizational politics. From there, a new threat emerges from the burgeoning Chiss Ascendancy, which has united the Unknown Regions under one rule. Surely, no Chiss would be foolish enough to attack Palpatine’s Empire. Yet, it seems one Chiss knows that the ambitions of an egotistical dictator know no bounds. Against the advice of his fellow Chiss, who have exiled him from Unknown Space - Grand Admiral Thrawn, formerly of the Galactic Empire, leads a one man crusade to destroy the Empire he once swore to serve. 

He is sorely outmatched.

Over the past decade, the Empire has successfully completed not just one but three Death Stars, rotating them in their operation. After Aalderaan, thankfully none of these superweapons had to be deployed in the field. Still, their location at any one time is always a high level military secret, for they dwarf any fleet foolish enough to stay and actually fight when one of these monstrous moons does make an appearance.

And yet, cracks in the Empire are beginning to show.

Unknown to most sapients of the Galaxy, due to poor maintenance of these highly expensive stations, only one station is fully operational. The others suffer from supply issues, rebel saboteurs, and aging technical malfunctions. More and more, it seems that whoever designed the Death Stars had purposely planted another glaring weakness: extraordinarily high upkeep costs.

Still, when you have the resources of a Galaxy at your command such matters are seemingly trivial.

Thanks to the Galactic Empire, no widespread military conflict has occurred since the destruction of the First Death Star. Palpatine did keep his word in one respect: there was *peace*. And yet, more and more planetary populations were squeezed by their Imperial Governors and Moffs, making life peaceful only in the same way as a slave: there is technically life, but the soul remains in chains.

The Imperial Navy, Army, and other military branches have expanded their scope roughly three times in order of magnitude over the past decade, fueled by military conscription of planets into the Stormtrooper and Navy corps. The Imperial Inquisitorius has shrunk, as fewer Jedi remain in the Galaxy. Most people believe that the old tales of the Jedi were nothing more than children’s fables, actively meant to mislead and nullify fledgling rebel upstarts. Rather than Vader, Palpatine now rules with the aid of his newly unveiled bride - Mara Jade, the Emperor’s Hand. Her 500 strong legion of force sensitive ‘children’ called Reapers is composed of dark side warriors rumored to be created through genetic manipulation of deceased force users' DNA, grown in cloning vats. Serving as the knives of the Empire, they are viewed as Jade’s personal enforcers on any planet they visit. Each is fanatically in love with Mara Jade and would die for her, and often do. 

It is against this backdrop that Lexi makes her appearance on a secluded backwater planet….

PART 1

 You come out into a forest clearing, though you sense to be on another planet given the completely novel appearance of the sky, and based on the new quality of the twisting alien trees around you. Somehow, you’re not just in a forest anymore, but in a deep thick jungle. There is no civilization anywhere to be seen, and worst of all - the Force is nowhere to be found. It is as if you sense no Force anywhere, as if it has died. Or you lost the ability to sense it. Both are awful choices, but left with no clue those are the only two disturbing thoughts running through your mind at the moment.

You stand up and wipe off your knees, as you look back in shock to see that one of the dark warriors has followed you through the original portal, which appears not fully shut as yet, time unslowing in the Jedi Temple still faintly visible within. The Sith warrior tumbles out of the cyan portal, but unlike you he executes a roll and comes up on both feet, facing you directly. An unignited lightsaber handle sits in his right palm, finger on the ignition. The portal at last closes with a WOOOSH-SNAP. The new fearsome fighter appears tall, at least 2 meters, possibly more, and broadly muscled, but, unlike his compatriots, lacking heavy armor. Could this be their leader? The Sith wears a deathly mask on his face, his voice metallic, cold and harshly grating as it emanates through the death mask. **“There is nowhere in this Galaxy to run Jedi—”** He stops, startling as you did. He seems confused suddenly, looking up at the sky.

**“Wait, where the heck am I? And how are you blocking my access to the Dark Side? Well, speak!”** He ignites the red blade and points it in your direction, his voice trembling. He seems to be much like an addict who just got cut off from his favorite drug supply. Out of nowhere, he starts making bestial grunting noises, and muttering to himself, **“My power… I need it… Give it to me! No no no, I MUST have more power…. MORE…. Where, has it gone, there’s just no way… it could be all gone like that… You stole it, give it back to me! Right now!”** His arm shakes and suddenly his muscled body begins sweating profusely, sweat running off in watery cascades off his muscled ridges. He is currently about 5 meters away, but he is unsteadily approaching, wavering on his legs. As he gets to about 1 meter out, you surely think he’s about to cut you down, but instead he just drops to the ground in a heap of convulsions and his saber deactivates as soon as he loses his grip. The man writhes on the ground for a full minute, having a violent seizure, and at last his eyes roll up into his head. He lays unmoving, square on his back, gurgled rasps coming from his death mask: hyperventilating, but his breaths sound weak and shallow. Perhaps, you think, the mask might be making it difficult to breathe.

The emptiness Lexi feels as she stands and surveys her new surroundings is almost oppressive. For most of her life she had been able to touch the Force and gather impressions from it, but even with the abundant life around her, she felt… nothing. *Did something happen to me in the World Between Worlds?* she wonders.

Lexi’s normal senses work perfectly, however, as she sees a pursuer cross through the portal before it closes. A look of horror crosses her expression as the warrior draws closer making his demands to which she has no answer. Instead she draws her own saber in preparation to defend herself and takes a few cautious steps back. Without the Force, she would only have her wits and her martial training to fall back on against this obviously larger and stronger opponent. There was also the option of running, but she doubted very much she’d get far running blindly on a planet she had no knowledge of.

Her horror turns to resolution as he too seems to feel the withdrawal of the Force. His reaction to his loss startles her as she realizes it was affecting him more acutely. With every step he grows weaker and more deranged as if withdrawn from a powerful drug. When he’s not even a meter away, he collapses into a violent seizure. She stares at him in shock for a long moment before she moves into action. While she knows only moments ago this man was set on killing her, it wasn’t in her to watch someone suffer when she could at least attempt to help them. 

With her saber once more clipped to her belt, Lexi kneels next to the warrior and lets her pack slide from her back. Surveying him she looks to see how to remove the mask; if it was actually a mask and not some sort of life support apparatus. Once she sees it’s safe to remove it she fumbles the mask off. Her next task leaves her heaving against his massive bulk as she tries to get him on his side, using her pack as a wedge at his back to keep him that way.

As she comes across his lightsaber, she clips it securely to her belt; intent on preventing him from attacking her with it again at least until she could determine her next steps. Checking his breathing once more, she starts the process of removing her cloak with the intention of putting it under his head. Her fingers come across the hold out blaster she had placed in one of the pockets, and she withdraws it, putting it behind her belt at the small of her back. Gently she lifts his head and puts the flatley folded robe under his head. Satisfied she’s done all she can for the moment, she starts a search to make sure the Sith warrior didn’t have any other surprises on his person. 

While she works and then waits to see if he'll recover, she hums softly. The old lullaby comes to her, its soothing tones she'd witnessed having settled even the most aggrieved youngling. If she had the use of the Force, she might have reinforced the calm elicited by the lullaby to the warrior. Then again… in the end, all her attempts to help him may ultimately be her own undoing.

Searching the unconscious man yields the following items: 2 lightsabers (red), 1 datapad (secured), 5000 credits on secured chip, 2 blaster pistols (holdout, concealed in his boot and belt), 1 multitool, 1 vibroblade, 1 identicard (secured), 1 death mask, 1 set of acolyte's robes, 8 military ration packs, 1 flask (half full), 1 rebreather, 1 utility belt.

content: Peeling back his mask seems to relax his tension only slightly. On the inside of the mask you also find an attached comlink, along with some kind of computer. His face is strikingly handsome, an unexpected result for sure, but not unwelcome. You remove the black Sith hood to find that the man bears long, dark and flowing hair. As you turn him over to one side, he seems to groan in pain. He begins to mumble his words unintelligibly. Sweat continues to pour off his brow, and whole body for that matter, soaking the makeshift pillow you provided him. He seems unable to come out of this state for about an hour, after which time the sweating stops, his heart rate slows, and the tension in his face finally eases up. His eyes gradually open up, face serenely looking up at the canopy above. Without his death mask, he somehow seems completely different from the homicidal man intent on murdering you just an hour before. He looks around, and doesn't appear to recognize you as his eyes take in the jungle and stop upon finding Lexi's young face looking back at him, **"Who are you? And where... am I? Feels strange... I think I must still be dreaming..."** He bends at the waist, sitting up. His muscles are gleaming from the sweat, and he seems completely serene, taking a deep breath in, inhaling the jungle air. He closes his eyes in ecstasy, **“I feel alive, rejuvenated…”** He opens his eyes and his face seems content, serene, at peace. **“That melody, it was… beautiful.”** He looks at Lexi intensely.

Removing the mask and hood concealing the Warrior’s face causes Lexi to pause in her melody as surprise overcomes her. Setting the mask aside to put the cloak beneath his head, she resumes her humming from the refrain, the melody easily repeating itself. She studies the man further, her earlier surprise now overcome. The Dark Side she had been led to believe, corrupted and scared its most avid followers, but his features seemed untouched. 

Lexi continues to hum as she removes the weapons from the Sith and with lack of an appropriate place to put them, finds a place on her belt for them. A part of her feels silly with how overladen with weapons she now was. Taking inventory of his other equipment, she leaves everything else in place, though she does take his flask and opens it to sniff at the contents suspiciously.

While she waits, Lexi paces around their small clearing, taking further stock of their surroundings and begins trying to determine which direction would be the best course. Paths made by animals might lead to water, which would be vital for an extended trek through the wilderness. In turn finding a water source might lead to finding a settlement. Once she’s satisfied she returns to sitting cross legged beside the Warrior on a patch of dried leaves she kicks into place. 

She holds up the mask, intrigued by the internal display for the computer, but doesn’t dare to put it on. Instead she turns her attention to the commlink, and begins to cycle through the channels. With the multitude of options, she doubts anything would come of it, but maybe the Force would be with her. Her tinkering with the commlink doesn’t get very far when the warrior begins to visibly relax and stabilize. When he opens his eyes, the young Jedi grows quiet and she shifts into a more ready position should she need to get up quickly. She’s placed herself just out of easy reach, but he could still make a lunge for her and she wants to be ready.

A part of her relaxes as she sees the shift in his countenance. “**I’m Lexi Ardena,**” she introduces herself, omitting her newly granted title. Using it here would grant her no favors.. With a small apologetic shrug she answers his next. “**I can’t tell you where we are. I don’t know myself.**” For the moment, she decides not to go into more detail of how they came about finding themselves in the secluded jungle. Instead she continues to watch him intently as he manages to sit up. “**This place is a void in the Force,**” Lexi offers to ease his mind regarding the strangeness of it. It seems a better and a more hopeful explanation than something had happened to them both when they came through the portal. “**I’m afraid I don’t know much more about where we are.**” *Or when,* she says silently, knowing full well they had not only been transported to a strange and distant world, but also into the future.

Lexi’s own intent gaze meets his and she marvels at his transformation. Just an hour before he had been homicidally deranged, intent on killing her, but now the serenity she finds on his face is unexpected. She nods her head slowly in acceptance of his compliment of the lullaby. “**Who are you?**” she asks, repeating his earlier question back at him.

The man winces at the question, his gaze turning internal and thoughtful, brow wrinkling. **“I am called Wraith, or Fresh Meat by my fellow Shades. I hold the rank of junior-most Acolyte in my squad.
