With a nod, Lexi stretches out her limbs from the lack of movement. "**Why don't you head for the command tent and I'll start sabotaging the bikes.**" she suggest. While she doesn't like the idea of splitting up, the less time spent in the camp the better.
**"Do you want me to take care of the two on duty?"**

She frowns, gauging the time before the next rotation. "**It's a risk either way, but not having them raise an alarm right away may buy us more time.**" Slowly she nods, "**We should take them out and hide them so they're not discovered right away.**" 
The rest of her plan after dispatching the pair of guards is to split up with Wraith proceeding to the command tent to collect what intel and supplies he can manage while Lexi uses her limited knowledge to sabatoge the bikes to at least delay any pursuit. She gathers any spare gear she finds on the bikes and moves it the closest speeder to the command tent. If there's time she also tries to free however many of the captured creatures she can from the frames, though she doesn't reveal that part of her plan to Wraith.

She rummages through the gear of the guards as well and takes one of their carbines in liue of the smaller blaster for their getaway and tosses anything else that might be useful into her pack for latter.

Wraith and Lexi nod and make their way stealthily into the Imperial camp. The Vornskrs seem to somehow intuitively understand that, based on your body language, you’re on the hunt, and also keep themselves preternaturally silent. Lexi and Wraith split off to each their assigned task. Wraith ends one Stormtrooper with a saber ignition to the back of the neck, while the second Stormtrooper on duty gets tackled by a Vornskr and neck cracked, but not before crying out in startled surprise. Lexi meanwhile goes off to begin disabling the antennas as described to her, and picking up the dead troopers’ equipment: finding 2 medpacks, 2 power packs, 2 comm units and 2 blaster carbines on the dead bodies. The camp begins to awaken, as half-dressed troopers emerge from the barrack tents guns blazing. Wraith is able to dispatch 3 more, before he is forced to retreat to the designated speeder by the command tent. Jumping astride, he helps pull Lexi aboard on his back, while deflecting blaster bolts with his red saber in his off hand. As soon as Lexi is aboard, Wraith hits the accelerator and with a whiplash the speeder bike takes off from a dead stop and rides through camp. Lexi, on the back seat, takes another shot, dropping 2 more troopers as they attempt a surprised pursuit on foot.

She has managed to steal a datapad from the command tent and find a map of the area, as she desperately yells directions to Wraith. Meanwhile, Wraith is gunning the speeder as fast as it can move. Unfortunately, the entire camp seems to be buzzing like a beehive and they are pursued by the Speeder Sled (3 stormtroopers occupying) and 1 Speeder Bike (1 scout trooper aboard). The motorized wheeled vehicles can’t keep up and get bogged down in the jungle foliage. Blaster bolts from the sled and bike whiz past, illuminating the path ahead. Most of the fire is coming from the sled where 2 stormtroopers are using their carbines to try to end your existence. After some fancy maneuvering, Wraith manages to outmaneuver the sled by passing in between two thick tree trunks, while the Sled explodes into a fireball going full speed in the dense jungle. The last Scout Trooper on speeder bike, however, is clearly not so easy to shake, scoring a hit on your speeder bike engine, which starts to fume spitting out black smoke. Thanks to his lighter load, the Scout Trooper ends up gaining on your speeder bike, neck in neck.

Lexi attempts to ignite her lightsaber, damaging the trooper’s engine mechanism, and his speeder bike does several flips and then disappears into a huge fireball. Unfortunately, your own joy ride comes to an end as your speeder bike sputters the last few gouts of black smoke, settling down to the ground with a mechanical death gasp. Hopping off the stolen speeder bike you both run full speed through the jungle, pursued by the distant sounds of revving motorcycle engines. After trying to avoid the floodlights of the motorcycles, which appear to be coordinated by a Stormtrooper Commander on quad-motorcycle, you find yourself hiding underneath a stump and breathing hard, looking into each other’s eyes as the adrenaline fades. The sounds of motorized pursuit gradually increase, as you hear loudspeaker enhanced commands emanating and echoing through the jungle. After holding your breath, the patrol seems to pass over you and go on in a different direction. Giving it a good 15 minutes, you both stand and decide to relocate to a new jungle area to try to lose your pursuit. However, just as soon as you attempt to escape the search area, you hear a shout and bodies illuminated by floodlights holding sensors point in your direction.

You run, heart beating faster than you’ve ever thought possible, run straight through the thickest jungle foliage on foot. You both sustain countless cuts and bruises, Lexi rolling her ankle, and Wraith having to carry her in his arms. Both of you have long bloody scratches from all the branches you have to run past. The floodlights and motorcycle revving sounds behind you get gradually louder and louder, as Wraith follows your directions, hastily memorized from the datapad stolen at the command tent. Finally, after 2 straight hours of pursuit you break into a clearing. Only to be greeted by an AT-ST walker, flanked by two dozen mobilized Stormtroopers, all pointing their rifles in your direction as if expecting you to be herded in this direction. Wraith sets you down and raises both hands in the air, realizing the game’s up. He is breathing hard and clearly disappointed at the way this turned out.

An Imperial officer emerges from the AT-ST, wearing a black Imperial officer’s uniform and looking with contempt at the pair. **“You’ve managed to give us quite the chase. Put them in cuffs and load them onto the Lambda shuttle, I’ve orders to take these two aboard the ISD.”** Deprived of your freedom, you’re both cuffed and transferred aboard a Lambda-class shuttle. Strangely, as soon as you’ve boarded the Lambda shuttle and left the surface of the planet, the Force has returned fully to both of you. Even though you find yourselves restrained, the old unexpected ally has returned just as mysteriously as it disappeared. The Lambda shuttle lands in the bay of an Imperial Star Destroyer. You haven’t had a chance to converse about a coordinated plan of attack, staring at each other’s eyes while both flanked by armed Stormtroopers. After touching down, the Stormtroopers escort you onto the landing deck of the ISD. You get to see a hint of green-blue, the planet below you. Strangely, the Force doesn’t give you dire warnings of impending execution and demise, as you would expect. Wraith seems to keep the Dark Side under control, having practiced self-control the past 3 days. You’re led down endless corridors through the guts of the ISD, for what feels like a half hour. You’re processed: DNA and vitals collected. Finally, you find yourself led into a large and ornate display room, some kind of Virtual Art Gallery you realize. With your hands cuffed behind your backs, you’re left alone in the large dimly lit display gallery. After wandering for 10 minutes, the double doors at the distant end of the gallery open and an Imperial Grand Admiral strides in, flanked by a Senior officer and a short furry alien with beady eyes. The trio stops, making direct eye contact with the duo of prisoners. The Grand Admiral speaks first, in a clear and commanding tone:

**“I’m Grand Admiral Thrawn, formerly of the Galactic Empire, and I need to know how much you know about my Ysalamiri operation. My commanders recommended executing you on the spot, but before I do so I need to know exactly how much the Empire knows.”** The Grand Admiral is clearly alien, with blue skin and glowing red eyes, but his verbal charisma is on another level altogether. His tone clearly carries the experience of years and decades in service. The red glowing eyes lock with Wraith’s first. 

Wraith attempts to meet the commanding gaze with dignity. **“I’m Wraith, and I’m no longer with the Empire. We overheard your minions collecting lizards on the planet’s surface, that’s all we know.”**

The white-uniformed officer nods expectantly, looking at his datapad in one outstretched hand, **“Yes… Wraith, we have confirmed that there’s a bounty on your head by the Reapers. Any reason I shouldn’t turn you in for a nice payday? And while you’re explaining your life to me, care to tell me who your enigmatic compatriot might be, her DNA has no Galactic records at all.”** The Grand Admiral turns his malevolent gaze at Lexi, studying her like a specimen on a dissecting table. **“We even ran genetic analysis and no citizens of the Empire even came close to matching as your relatives. Most intriguing, I should say.”** He shakes the datapad in Lexi’s direction. **“If I were a betting man, I’d venture to say you to be a Jedi, but everyone knows they’re extinct.”**

Lexi watches the exchange keeping a neutral expression all the while trying not to think about her throbbing ankle. Following Wraith's lead, she cooperates with the examination and relies on the Force to sustain her exhausted and sore body. She doesn't miss the mention this ship and it's crew was formerly of the  Empire. 
"**I won't be in your system as my world isn't part of the Empire...  My world was forgotten eons ago and left to fend for ourselves. Where I'm from the Jedi do still exist.**" She glances at Wraith, knowing it was possible his squad had made there way to the academy and killed everyone.  Unconsciously she squares her shoulders as she meets the Admiral's crimson gaze."**My name is Lexi Ardena, Knight of the Jedi Order of Tython.**"
The Chiss seems taken aback at the prideful declaration, tilting his head to one side in bemusement, **"Are you now..."**

The Grand Admiral furrows his brows, not liking the sound of subordination in the tone of his prisoners, **"You're quite bold, claiming yourself to be a member of an extinct Order, knowing yourself to be in the clutches of the Imperial War Machine, and yet..."** He smiles openly, showing perfectly white teeth, adding to his sinister facade, **"Boldness in stating the Truth, that is something this dying Galaxy badly needs..."** He walks up closer within 5 meters to Lexi and inspects her from looking down his nose. **"You really are a Jedi, aren't you... For a decade your kind have been extinct, and yet here you just show up out of the blue on my ship... I wonder if this is what your kind would call, the Will of the Force."**

Lexi tracks the strange blue skinned alien as he draws closer, her lips pursed as she unabashedly keeps his gaze.  It was a risk keeping to the truth, but he had already made it clear they were considering executing them. She inclines her head slightly in acknowledgement of his statement. "**How is it you know so much about the Jedi, if as you say they're extinct?**"

The Grand Admiral gestures around the art gallery, **"I study many things, especially the former enemies of the Galactic Emperor. Palpatine considered your kind dangerous above all."** He grasps his chin in one hand, regarding you with interest twinkling in his eyes.

"**It's because he fears what we represent... Or at least what we once did.**" Lexi watches him with equal interest. "**Justice... truth...**" She pauses before adding, "**Hope. It's all things his regime lacks and what the people desire. He knew the Jedi would be the only force capable of countering him so he turned the people against us and launched a campaign to annihilate us.**"

**"Indeed. That was more or less my conclusion as well."** As he walks around you two, you both realize he emanates a Force-absent bubble, gesticulating at the air as he talks, **"I've recently discovered a way to defend against the Emperor and his Reapers. Everyone thinks they're invincible due to their Dark Side abilities, but that is not so. Thanks to some meticulous esoteric research, my sources have learned that this planet, called Myrkr, is home to a special species of lizards called Ysalamiri, capable of pushing away the energy field referred to as the Force."** He smiles wickedly, enjoying his height advantage. **"I need operatives capable of helping me bring down the Empire, and you two seem to be prime candidates at the moment: one extinct Jedi and one excommunicated Reaper. If you join my... organization, you'd agree to be hunted by the Galaxy at large, but you'd be fighting for something more important than any mere material object - you'd be helping me fight to restore freedom, justice, and truth to the entire Galaxy. So... what say you, exiles."**

**"I see that I wasn't the only one brave enough to make the decision to defect. I don't say this often, Admiral, but your trails of thought mirror my own, alarmingly I might add. We're both Imperials who were disillusioned by the service foisted upon our shoulders."** He reaches out to shake Thrawn's hand, **"It would be my absolute honor to bring down the whole damn Empire, crash it to the ground, and burn it down to ashes."**
The Grand Admiral's red eyes twinkle and a sly grin touches his expression, **"I like you already. And what say you, Jedi?"**
Wraith had tried to reach out to shake Thrawn's hand, but found himself stopped by his solid cuffs.

Lexi listens, her suspicions regarding the lizard creatures being the source of the void in the Force of the planet confirmed. His proposition in their current status as prisoners suddenly seems ridiculous to her. She gives a small hiccup as she contains the sudden laughter boiling up inside her. "**Honestly?... It's hard to agree to anything when we're your prisoners.**"

The Chiss glides up cooly to Lexi's personal space, and holds up his datapad in front of her face. You witness him, with an exaggerated motion, point to his datapad and flip a digital switch from 'red' to 'green' and your handcuff light indicators beep, matching the color change. Your cuffs fall off and clang to the floor, both your's and Wraith's.
**"I'm not the Empire, you'll soon find. I operate my organization on a higher level of efficiency than that rotten tyrannical bureaucracy. I only work with free sapients."**

The first hint of annoyance crosses her features for a split second before relief floods through her freed muscles as the restraints clatter to the deck. She rubs her wrists in an effort to quickly restore the circulation. Shifting slightly she casts her gaze to Wraith who had already thrown in his lot with Thrawn so quickly. He was her only ally, and now it seemed the only way she'd be able to find her way in this unknown galaxy was to throw her lot in with him as well. "**It so happens that our goals align...**" She pauses deciding how best to present her conditions. "**I have my own mission that one day I'll have to set out to complete.**"
"**Until then... I'll join you in the fight against the Empire.**"

Grand Admiral Thrawn gives a subcentimeter bow to you, **"Glad to have a Jedi on our side, even if it's for a short time. I believe each of us understand each other, now."** He walks towards the senior Imperial officer and gives a command, **"Captain Pelleon, please escort our new allies to their officers quarters. Give them Room 703A and attach them to Longsword Squadron. Issue them IDs, officer stipend, and assign both to work under my direct command."** He halts, back to you both, and turns his head, **"Rest well, you'll be hearing from me."**

The mustached senior officer does as commanded, and you soon find yourself sitting in your luxurious officers quarters across from Wraith, who has bunked up on top and seems to be daydreaming, hands crossed behind the back of his head.

Having done her very best to not limp on their way to their new berth, she now collapses into one of the chairs before starting the process of removing her boot and stocking to assess the damage. It had been a very long day and with the advent of the return of their Force abilities was eager to meditate in its embrace.
"**Things certainly have worked out for us...**" She comments as she rubs her now bare foot.  "**I know you believe otherwise... But I do believe the Force has guided us here.**"
**"Who would have thought we'd run into Thrawn like that. Let's just say... I'm starting to come around."**

She casts a glance over at him and gives him a grin. "**Well .. if you're impressed, then he must be somebody important. I suppose the question is then: why would he, of all people with power in the Empire, defect?**"

**"Suppose we'll have to ask him one of these days, but something tells me that type of... being... doesn't appreciate following strict guidelines from authority to be followed blindly and unquestioningly."**

He pulls up a schematic of a TIE Defender, rotating in 3 holodimensions. **"Longsword Squadron, I like the sound of that... Some kind of new experimental TIE Fighter, with shields and hyperspace capability."**
Lexi looks dubiously at the holo projection. "**Means I'll have to learn to pilot a fighter doesn't it?**" It wasn't a task she was look forward to.

She grows quiet for a moment as something else comes to the surface that's been bothering her. "**I've been wondering... How far into the future did I come? I get the feeling we're not too far off from when you came. Long enough for you to be marked a renegade and not declared dead ...**"

**"Sorry Jedi Princess, but up here in the real world, we can't all just teleport wherever we need to go."** He stops, considering her question, **"By my count it's been 1 month after I tried entering the past, your past."**

She ignores his obvious jibe. There wasn't going to be anymore travel into the World Between Worlds for a long time. If ever again. Instead she focuses on getting her answer. "**One month for you... And for me? Do you know how far back they sent you?**"
**"Something like 9 year if I'm not mistaken. Why, does that hold some significance?"**

"**I don't know,**" she says evenly. It mattered to her though as it was time she lost, but with that lost time she had survived. The paradox of time travel threatened to give her a headache. She looks away, off into space as she softly explains, "**It's nine years of a timeline that converged from what should have been. When the Chosen One was killed before he could fulfill his destiny and bring about balance.**" She pauses and looks back up at Wraith. "**I don't know when or how .. but a clone of Palpatine went into the past before the Battle of Endor and changed the course of things. That's what I want to stop and why I came to this time... To fix the past... My time. I had to come to the future.**"

Wraith furrows his eyebrows, straining to think. **"A Clone of Palpatine you say? I believe it used to take 10 years to grow a fully mature clone, using old cloning techniques. Maybe that's why the Force sent you 9 years into the future, to try to stop this Palpatine Clone from being born. If true, then you haven't got much time to locate and exterminate the Palpatine Clone that will change the course of History."**
He adds, **"Your History, anyway."**

Lexi nods his words making sense. "**Would he use the old methods though if clones can be created faster? For all we know the clone has already been born and just waiting for the correct time to go back.**"

**"It's possible, technologically certainly, but any alteration to the sequence of temporal events stands a chance of introducing timeline variable. What if the hyperaccelerated cloning technique introduces a tumor into the Clone's brain? I doubt the Emperor is one to take needless risks."**
"**Okay then.**" She pauses as she considers the relatively daunting task. "**So I have less than a year to find this clone.**"
"**Perhaps it's all the better we're now working with Thrawn. Surely he'll have the resources and perhaps the desire to help me with this.**"

Wraith smiles, **"Shouldn't take long to find... If I'm recalling correctly, you never asked where I was born. The answer is, a top secret research facility called Mount Tantis. Problem is, no one knows the location of this facility, especially not I."**

"**Mount Tantis,**" she repeats. "**And all the clones are born there?**" If true it was something to start with and the new bits of information, while not conclusive, gives her a lightness to her bearing. She puts her chin in her hand as she rests her elbow on the arm of the chair.
"**We just need to find someone who does know... Maybe a particular Grand Admiral was privy to such information.**"
**"Sorry but I definitely don't know about any other secret cloning facilities... just mine."**
**"I doubt Thrawn is even aware they exist, most beings in the Galaxy aren't."**

"**Aware of there being clones or of the facilities?**" She asks for clarification knowing the answer was likely to be both. "**If that's the case... Then it may prove harder to convince Thrawn to look for Mount Tantis. You're my only proof and even then .. is there anything that might reveal the cloning process? They looked at your DNA and didn't mention anything was amiss afterall.**"

**"Oh no they certainly realized I was a clone, or genetically engineering being, it's hard not to come to that conclusion. The existence of cloning technology is not in doubt, given we had a whole conflict called the Clone Wars. The question is, whether that technology was lost or remains to this day."**
**"Supposedly somewhere within my DNA is a hard-encoded 'birth date', never bothered to track down that particular rumor though."**
"**And what exactly is that?**"

**"A date of conception, manufacture date one might call it. Though its probably encrypted, along with a serial number, in case they find a blood smear and need to conclude which of their Reapers left it."**

Lexi makes a face at the dehumanizing aspect of it all. "**If they have the ability then someone else out there probably knows how to find it out as well.  Just a matter of finding them.**"
Slowly she stands on her bare feet, putting the least amount of weight on her sprained foot as she hobbles to the bed, eager to lay down at last. She sighs contentedly as she sprawls out on the lower bunk. "**The Force has guided us this far,**" she says sleepily, before covering a sudden yawn.

"**Let's get some sleep and see whats in store for us tomorrow.**" She rolls over to find a comfortable position. "**At least we're not going to freeze tonight,**" she jokes lightly. Her breathing slows as she focuses on entering a healing force trance.
Thus ends the third day.

Lexi wakes and without the mundane methods of the sky to tell her the time she shifts on the bunk to glance at the chrono on a nearby table. The sounds she’d grown accustomed to on a planet were replaced by the new sounds of the ship’s thrumming, a rather disconcerting switch as she considers the change in the Force to accompany it. While on Myrkr, she hadn’t been able to touch the Force and sense the life around her, and while there was life on the ship, it wasn’t in the form of the expansive fauna and flora, but now the thousands of sentient lives crammed into one metal shell. 

Knowing her movements would not go unnoticed by the hyper-vigilant Sith in the bunk above her, Lexi remains where she is. Slowly she stretches each of her muscles and takes stock of any remaining injuries she sustained during their flight the previous day. The scratches and bruises were all but healed thanks to her time in the trance leaving just her sprained ankle. Tentatively she rolls it around and is satisfied when she gets only a minor twinge. 

With a sigh she contents herself with taking the time to recuperate and meditate, something that was lacking the last few days as she and Wraith traveled across Myrkr. She wonders what the Vornskr pups were doing, having been separated from them during their flight and eventual capture. Lexi hopes they’re unharmed and make their way back to their pack and not waiting for her and Wraith to return.

As the chrono hits 0600, Lexi sits up in the bunk and pads as softly as she can to where she left her pack the previous evening. The equipment she had taken from the guards was confiscated, which doesn't bother her too much, but as she shifts through the contents she does a quick inventory of the scattered possessions to make sure there was nothing else. Taking a change of clean clothing from her pack in the process she heads for the attached refresher, a perk of the officer cabin she supposes, and proceeds to wash away the grime and blood before changing into the clean clothes. In the light of the refresher she examines the torn, dirty clothing wondering how she was going to go about remedying their state. She folds them into a neat stack, goes back into the main room, and puts them into one of the storage lockers to deal with later.

Sitting comfortably in one of the luxurious chairs she flips through the tome Davian had given her while she waits for Wraith to take care of his own needs. 
""**Morning,**"Lexi says, brightly. "**Do you know where  we are supposed to go to get food?**" she asks at last. "**A mess hall?**" They hadn't eaten since before their attempt to raid the camp and her own supplies were now depleted. She was wary of roaming the ship on her own, though she was loathe to admit it.
At exactly 0600 the ship lights flare on and Reveille is sounded.

Lexi and Wraith both awaken, freshen up individually, and make ready to head for the Mess Hall, as directed to them by the wall holopanel. Just as they are standing to go, boots can be heard clanging off durasteel walkways outside of your officers quarters at 0700. Without any warning, your door slides open and an Imperial Officer steps inside, dressed in an unconventional Imperial Uniform, flanked by four stormtroopers. You recognize him as Captain Pellaeon, who appears as an aging man with white mustache and big wide face, blue piercing eyes, and the dignity of a lifelong Imperial Naval Officer.

**“Rise and shine, lazy Force users. Grand Admiral Thrawn requests your presence on the bridge of the Chimaera. Follow me. That was a command, in case I wasn’t being clear.”** The four blaster carbines lower in unison, pointing in your general direction. It’s clear there is no room for disagreement, judging by the seriousness of the Captain’s tone. Of course, you could always use the Force to make a show of Force, but… you wonder if this is the time or place for that. For now, at least, you’ve found yourself in the good graces of the Chiss. The Captain executes a crisp turnabout and marches off towards the ship’s aft turbolifts. The stormtroopers wait for you two to follow in his footsteps, and take position close behind, their carbines held at parade attention in both hands as they march to follow the three officers.

Without even looking backwards, he begins a hefty tirade at your expense, **"Just because the Admiral has taken an interest in you, that doesn't change what we of the True Empire feel for your kind. You're dangerous, undisciplined, unstable, and above all - unreliable."*** He turns in the corridor expertly avoiding an oncoming formation of Cadets jogging somewhere in the opposite direction. You find the speed of his stride intimidating, and can barely keep up with the old man. **"Every Force user I've ever seen is addicted to its power, as it corrupts and twists all of you into murderous psychopaths, until you inevitable destroy yourselves, and any misfortunate military personnel in your immediate surroundings."** He halts, having arrived at the double doors to the Bridge. Turning around, he gives you both an intimidating stare, **"When you two inevitably decide to self destruct and it comes time for your heads to pop like overripe jackfruit. Just don't let it be anywhere near me."**
The doors to the Bridge slide open.

The young Jedi can't help but respond to the captain's tirade with a raised eyebrow. She had expected some animosity and distrust, but to actually be confronted with it was going to take some getting used to. As they walk she tries to get the lay of the ship, but the myriad of identical corridors without her familiar references soon as her list. 
Now outside the doors, she risks a sidelong glance at Wraith before she turns her attention back to Palleon "**Noted, Captain,**" she responds simply, before heading onto the bridge through the open doors where she assumes Thrawn is waiting for them.

You enter at the Bridge double doors, guarded by two Death Troopers, who sternly scrutinize you with green glowing lenses. They demand to check everyone's identification, before allowing admittance. Once inside, you find yourself at the bottom of the bridge pit, where numerous Imperial personnel are running around and rapidly tapping at keypads. You are led upstairs to the Elevated Walkway, where at the rear of the atrium, the White Command Chair stands majestically, occupied by the Chiss Grand Admiral Himself. A small family of orange lizards is nested at the apex of the Command Chair, which appears as a miniature terrarium, complete with twigs, leaves, water and food dispensary. The Chiss eyes the three officers as you approach. **"Status report, Captain Pellaeon."** He seems to ignore the Force users, choosing instead to converse with the Captain.

**"Our ground troops have been withdrawn from the surface, along with all respective equipment, and the base was demolitioned just as commanded, sir. The Chimaera has been withdrawn to higher orbit and is ready to enter hyperspace, at your order. It will take approximately 20 minutes to bring all of our engines to full hyperspace ready status."**
**"Excellent. So, Captain, why aren't my engines warming up as we speak?"**

**"Admiral, we are still counting our specimens this morning and making sure we have enough Ysalamiri, since many do not appear to have survived forced resettlement inside of artificial Nutrient Frames. Shall I have your permission to order one additional sweep of the area for specimen collection?"**

**"I see. Prepare a report on the survival rate of transferred Ysalamiri and have it on my holodesk in an hour. Then, I shall decide what to do about our... scaly friends."** He gestures at the lizards behind him.
Lexi, at the end of that pronouncement, the Force gives you a Very Bad Premonition. Something bad is going to happen, imminently.

Lexi listens with mild interest, her attention mostly focused on the small pack of the Ysalmiri Thrawn was keeping close to him. As the feeling of dread comes to her through the Force her attention immediately goes to Thrawn trying to ascertain if he was the source before looking about her.

Nothing obvious seems out of place, the bridge is mildly busy with morning activity, but no threat is visible. And yet, deep in the pit of your stomach, you know a terrible danger is approaching. You wonder whether you should interrupt the Captain to let the Admiral know, but you're not quite sure what to say, or how to even say it.
The Sith seems suddenly on alert, as well, looking around the bridge and scanning everyone in turn, his eyes darting quickly side to side.

"**There's something coming,**" she says in low tones possibly only Wraith can hear her. "**Something bad is about to happen.**" Without being able to pinpoint where the feeling is coming from she looks over at Wraith who she notices also growing restless.
The Grand Admiral has shifted his eyes to the two Force users, as your demeanor had changed. **"Lieutenant Ardena, Lieutenant Kai. Do you have something to share with the rest of the class?"**

The pit in her stomach from the impending doom causes her to hesitate for a moment.  In any other company she wouldn't have hesitated to express the impressions she was feeling, but here amongst those who doubted her intentions. Not looking at Palleon she keeps her focus in Thrawn. "**We're sensing impending danger,**" she says after her brief moment of hesitation.
**"Something's coming. Like a Nexu sensing the coming of a Rancor."**

**"Oh please, spare me. Not even aboard the bridge for but a minute, and already filling it to the brim with mystical hogwash."** He rounds on you two. **"This is the bridge of an Imperial Star Destroyer, not a fortune teller stand at the underground bazaar. Here, we follow Military Protocol, not Parlor tricks."**

Lexi all but ignores Pallaeon as she looks around the bridge once more, reaching out will all her senses to determine the source of the danger. "**The Admiral asked us a question, Captain. I imagine not doing so would have against Military Protocol as well?**"
The Captain's face reddens at the rebuke, and just as he's about to launch into another tirade, an outstretched hand from Thrawn halts the Captain's next inevitable outburst.

**"Peace, Captain. Let them speak. I value the opinions of all my crew members, Force sensitive or not. It was once said that the Jedi of old could foretell events in the unfolding of history, moments before they occurred. Tell me, Lieutenants, what course of action would you have me take in light of this... imminent danger."**

Still unable to pinpoint the source of the danger she looks over at Wraith to see if he had any additional insight.  Without anything to add she looks back to Thrawn. "**Without knowing exactly what is about to happen I suggest either prepare for a fight or leave. The sooner the better.**"

The Captain's face reddens even more, his great white moustache twitching with impotent rage, **"Respectfully, Admiral, I don't even know why you bother to have these psi-addicts aboard our proud vessel. Clearly, they've nothing but manipulations and mind games in store for us."**

Continuing to hold up one hand at Pellaeon's objections, he casually plucks the arm console of the Command Chair bringing up a tactical holoschematic of the local sector, with the Chimaera as a bright white triangle at the center of the holoprojection.
The Projection is the size of a conference table, with Thrawn at the head and the three officers at the perimeter.
**"It appears there is nothing to fight, nor anything to flee from, based upon the latest sensor sweep."**
**"Of course not, Admiral. They're just trying to jockey for power, jumping at shadows, like their kind always does. HARUMPH~"**
The feeling of dread in your stomach is getting stronger.

**"Well, just in case, it wouldn't hurt to spool up the engines a bit early, in case someone does arrive to intend us harm."** Thrawn flicks a comm switch on his Captain's Chair armrest, **"Navigation, ready hyperspace engines and plot navicoordinates for  the nearest uninhabited system coreward. Prepare to execute hyperspace jump on my mark."** A few seconds later, in the crew pit a head comes up, shouting up in your general direction: **"Acknowledged, Admiral. Droids are computing coordinates now. Engines warming up as ordered."**

**"Observe carefully, Captain Pellaeon. If our guests are wrong, we'll have lost nothing but a few minutes of nav calculation time, but if they're right... well, a slight wrinkle of effort may save us from a tsunami of troubles."**

Lexi looks over the projection for a moment noticing too there seens to be nothing if immediate interest.  Taking in a breath she lets it out slowly as she closes her eyes and focuses only on the Force. *The Force is with me,
And I am one with the Force;* Her lips move silently to the first part of the mantra. She intends on letting the Force guide her into revealing the source of her and Wraith's premonition.

You close your eyes and immediately a Force vision swims up to the surface of your consciousness, you picture a brief glimpse of a predator in the jungle foliage, preparing to strike at unsuspecting prey.
"**The planet!**" She says, leaning heavily on the table in front of her as the vision overtakes her senses. "**Your men on the planet are in danger!**"
**"Ridiculous, we've abandoned the surface while you slept, and now our men are all safely aboard."**

A strident female voice resounds from the crew pit. **"Comm, Sensor readings detected. Multiple large capital class ships are exiting light speed ahead of our exit vector. Superdreadnought class signature readings detected, this can't be right--"**
**"It's a whole damn fleet!"**

Lexi's eyes open, her visage one of confusion as she tries to decipher what it was the Force was trying to show her. If the planet was now abandoned, then why would it show her such an obscurity. Then her focus comes to rest on the officer and it becomes clear. They had been the unsuspecting prey, and the trap was sprunge.
**"Navigations, I want my sublight engines firing on the double! Angle us away from Myrkr and out of the direct path of the oncoming fleet. Time to completed nav calculcations!?"**
**"2.2 Minutes Admiral. Droids are rerunning calculations now."**
**"I count 3, now 4 Star Destroyers and 1 Interdictor. Is that the Constrictor? I believe it is..."**
**"Captain Harkoth at the helm, no doubt. We've got  two and a half minutes, tops, before the Gravity Wells are fully operational, Admiral."**

The Chimaera shudders as sublight engines kick on, rocking some people off their feet in the crew pit. **"Shields up. Sound General Alert throughout the ship."** A deep, resonating siren starts up in the background.

Lexi continues to cling to the edge of the projector table watching the ships appear on the sector display in various shapes in their shades of red. Unfamiliar with space combat she watches from her place at the table.
**"Aye aye, shields at 97%. Alert to General Quarters. TIE Defender pilots to your stations."**
Green turbolaser fire begins to rain down on the Chimaera, rocking the ship even further as the Admiral drives it further away from Myrkr.
**"Admiral! Calculations read as green! Ready to hyperjump on your mark."**

**"Execute hyperspace jump on my mark."** He pauses to grin at the Constictor. **"Mark."** The whole bridge shudders and the Chimaera jumps into hyperspace, as the stars elongate into starlines and a wave of nausea sweeps through everyone's guts.

As suddenly as it began, it was all over. The bridge was no longer being bombarded with green turbolasers and everyone stood looking around dumbfounded. From deep within the crew pit, a small wave of applause starts, builds up and eventually the entire bridge is resounding with applause.

**"Back to your Duty Stations! This isn't a horse racing arena, everyone remain on High Alert until we've made several quick and calculated hyperjumps to lose any pursuit. Then I want an efficiency report from each Crew Chief on the double! That was cutting it way too close for the Admiral's comfort, you lazy sniveling monkey-lizards!"** He storms off into the crew pit to berate some poor soul.

Looking a bit green, this being her first time making a jump into hyperspace, Lexi swallows heavily as the combined dread from the Force vision and now the new sensations of the hyperspace jump make her belly do flips. She doesn't relax as the tension in the bridge does, still unsettled by recent events.

**"It seems we all owe you our lives, Lieutenant Ardena, Lieutenant Kai."** He unfolds his hands, **"I don't suppose you're going to reveal the methods, but I would venture a guess as to say that the Force was behind your extrasensory abilities, am I close?"**
Wraith suddenly appears at Lexi's side and covers her hand in his, with an assuring nod.

Lexi hears the Admiral's question but doesn't immediately answer. She takes a breath as she looks first up a Wraith returning his nod with a small half smile. Her hand shifts to give his a small squeeze before letting it go and turns to look at Thrawn. "**Yes, all beings can get impressions from the Force. Usually feelings of deja vu or gut feelings. We've been trained to be more in tune with them and have better success acting on it.**"
She looks over at Wraith with a frown. "**The Force tried to show me what was about to happen, but my interpretation was incorrect. I saw a predator in the jungle about to attack it's prey.**"
In the background, a recording can be heard repeating, **"General Quarters, General Quarters, set Condition 1 throughout the ship. General Quarters, General Quarters, set Condition 1 throughout--"**

Thrawn flicks a switch at his side, and the message switches to, **"Set Condition 3 throughout the ship. Essential crew to stations, shift crew on standby."** Standing up from his command chair, Thrawn annonces: **" X.O, Captain Pellaeon. You have the bridge. Remain at Condition 3 until we make 4 microjumps, return to Condition 4, then set course for rendezvous with The Death's Head and The Inexorable in Sector 34... And finally... assign a Commendation to the Navigation Officer on duty."**

**"Aye, Admiral, I have the bridge. Nav Officer, everyone one more round of applause."** Pellaeon shouts afterwards: **"Everyone back to work, start compiling a list of vessels in that fleet, I want to know where each of those pampered Imps powder their noses!"**
Thrawn turns to address his Lieutenants, **"Follow me to my Conference Room, it's just aft of the Bridge. We can talk more inside."**

The Admiral's personal Conference Room is the one that holds the works of holoart Lexi and Wraith saw when they first made a truce with the ex-Imperial Chiss. Now they once again find themselves among its mysterious alien works of art.

**"I rather find that studying the art of a species, helps me to understand their innermost thought processes. It took me a long time to understand humans, but it seems like I always have so much more to learn about Humanity... It is truly no accident of History that your kind rose to dominate the greatest portion of the known Galaxy."**

Thrawn stares at Lexi, as if studying a bug on the dissecting table, **"You intrigue me, Jedi. I don't know if you realize what just happened, but had that Imperial Interdictor gotten just a few more seconds, we'd all be sucking on vacuum instead of... enjoying a nice view of Michelangelo and Da Vinci's greatest works."**
Thrawn gestures at the art gallergy, which has updated with the works of the Italian sculptors.

As Thrawn gestures to the artwork her gaze naturally goes to the holographic representations of the work of art. Anywhere seemed preferable than to meet the uncomfortable crimson gaze of the intrigued Chiss. "**I'm not so proud I wont admit my understanding of what just happened is beyond me,**" she says evenly. "**It's perhaps why the Force tried to warn me the way it did.**"

**"A useful trait, more so if properly cultivated. Let me be frank. I want to train you both, and eventually give you command over your own Operational Unit. You'll have autonomy and could each act as you wish, within the parameters of your mission orders. There's a problem, however. You're both too green. First things first, you'll have to submit yourselves to a rigorous learning curriculum, to mesh yourself well with the men at my command. I want you to submit yourselves to 6 weeks of Imperial training."** He smirks, **"Don't worry, your instructor won't be Captain Pellaeon, I've other tasks in mind for him. I'll be sure to assign you to men more even tempered and respectful."** He shrugs his shoulders, **"Soon word will spread throughout the ship that you were the ones to warn me of the danger, and maybe the men will come to see you as more than just Reapers. You have to understand many of them served at the terror of the Reapers and naturally have hesitations about working with you. By virtue of being Force users you'll have to work hard to earn the trust of the men at my command, but... today wasn't a bad start."**

He rubs his chin, **"It also doesn't hurt your reputation that I took the liberty of suppressing the reports of your killing my troopers on the surface of Myrkr. Though you'll likely not find any allies among the Ground Corps, I should warn you now."**
**"Admiral, they attacked us first, we were just practicing self defense."**
**"I understand, of course. Acceptable casualties, given that you both saved everone onboard just now."**

Lexi's gaze falls back on Thrawn, her expression one of surprise before she covers it with one of practiced neutrality.  It was six weeks less she would have to investigate her own mission, but perhaps in the end would be to her advantage. Knowing nothing of the galaxy and the Empire was not her advantage.
She winces as her thoughts are interrupted by the Admiral's declaration and Wraith's defense.
"**These missions I can presume would be against Palpatine and the Empire? Military targets?**"

His red eyes flare with malice, **"Naturally they would be against our old foe, the Galactic Empire. However, no one said you'd only be going after military targets. You understand, of course, that anybody who supports the war efforts of the Empire, is our enemy."**
**"And is therefore by extension, a legitimate target for prosecution."**
"**And by prosecuting them, how does that make you any different those you're fighting against?**" She asks, undeterred.
**"Simple. We are different because we are fighting to free them from the shackles of barbaric tyranny, not trying to keep them in chains."**
"**How do you plan on freeing them if you make them a target? When you start targeting the people you're no better than the tyrant you wish to overthrow.**"

**"Yes, touché. You're quite gifted, young lady. You're correct of course, casualties of war are inevitable, demonstrated only too well by your killings of... Oh let me see..."** He pulls up his datapad, **"Ensign Criff Dannik, Petty Officer Bren Kargith..."** He runs down the list of about a half dozen casualties. **"I find the exchange of moral lectures between warriors tedious don't you?"**

Stymied as he lists the names of those their actions on Mykyr had ended in their deaths, her expression falls back to practiced neutral. "**My intent wasn't to lecture, Admiral. My apologies.**" She lets out a small breath as a sigh. "**I agreed to help you fight Palpatine. I won't, however, blatantly attack targets that are third party to his regime. Not without exploring other avenues first.**"

**"Very good, I think we have come to an understanding."** He replaces his datapad in the holster on his hip. **"In short order, I shall be assigning you to Basic Imperial Officer Training, accelerated, naturally, to 6 weeks. You'll both be expected to pass the final exam, administered by each respective instructor. Classes will include Imperial Military Doctrine, Mathematics, Gun Range Practice, Engineering, Computing, Piloting with Simulators, and... your favorite I'm sure, Moral Philosophy."**
He halts to look Lexi square in the face, **"If you want to beat the Empire, you first have to understand your enemy."**

**"Learn the ways of the Empire, and you shall learn how your enemy thinks. Palpatine is no fool, and only a determined opposition unfettered by moral ankle snares will ultimately triumph over the might of his Empire."**
**"Six weeks? That sound tedious, Admiral. Can't you give me a pass on account of my Reaper training?"**

**"No, Lieutenant Kai, your training and indoctrination was distinct and separate from standard Imperial Training. You were brainwashed and therefore never had to do any actual hands on learning, you shall find there are stark differences between embryonic indoctrination and actual, real life schooling."**
**"You make the rules, sir. I'll pass the training no problem, just worried that our sheltered Jedi girl over here won't make the cut, ya know?"**
Lexi nods in acquiescence to the plan Thrawn lays out. She turns to frown at Wraith. "**You underestimate my resolve. Besides I've been studying and passing trials since I was a child.**"

Thrawn brings a knuckle to his teeth and chuckles at that, **"Sheltered but not exactly helpless, it would seem... It seems you're both agreed on your next task. This shall also give me time to plan out your missions, specifically taking into account... your personal talents."**

He strokes his chin, **"Think of the possibilities, commanding a task force of Force users traveling around the galaxy, enacting my plans. I will promise if you follow my exact instructions, we stand a greater than 22% chance of defeating the Galactic Empire. Well, 21.75% chance technically speaking, but who's keeping score."**
Lexi raises a questioning eyebrow at his statement. "**A task force consisting of Force users? You've managed to recruit more than just the two of us?**"

Thrawn shrugs, **"Let's just say I like to hedge my bets, even when it comes to Force sensitives. Battle Group 2 is led by a Force user. I lead Battle Group 1. There are only two Battle Groups in all, but we're always looking to add more."**
Lexi nods slowly and glances at Wraith for a moment. She keeps her idea to herself for the moment unsure exactly of the viability with her limited knowledge. "**How soon will we begin?**"
**"Immediately starting tomorrow. At this time its past mid day on the ship, you may be dismissed, unless you have any final burning questions."**
"**No, sir,**" Lexi says simply and with a bow of her head turns to leave.

**"Very well then, you're both dismissed. Rukh, escort them back to their quarters."** For the first time in the long conversation, it becomes evident there was a fourth being inside the room the whole time. The short alien with beady eyes from yesterday comes out of the shadows at Thrawn's command, whisper-girgling, **"Hrrrryesssthrrr, Grrrrand Athmirrrral..."** The short grey creature looks hideous like a naked grey bat, but with spindly muscular limbs instead of wings.

A thought then comes to her as they're instructed to return to their quarters. "**I suppose there is one thing, Admiral; are we restricted to our quarters?**" She looks over at Wraith, neither of them having gone anywhere but their quarters, the bridge and Thrawn's office. "**While we've only been here a short time, it seems we've gone no where else without an escort.**"
**"You're free to wander the Unrestricted Hallways, but I'm not permitting you into High Security Areas yet, until you've passed your final exams at the very least."**

She nods appeased by his answer and turns to follow the strange being, Rukh, back to their quarters through the confusing array of identical corridors. 

 "**How do you keep track of where you are?**" She asks under her breath as she walks beside Wraith. "**This place is like a maze. Every corridor seemingly the same as the last.**"
**"Here you harrrrr... I shall returrrrrrn to my duties now, humansssss..."** Seemingly with innate ability to navigate the durasteel wilderness, the shady creatures has led you back home.
You arrive at your quarters, happy to discover someone has already ordered you a full meal. You dig in as you arrive back to Room 703A.

Happy enough to be back, Lexi heads to the table with the tray of food and collapses into the seat. "**The morning has proven to be interesting to say the least, **" she starts conversationally. "**It seems we're going to have our work cut out for us with this training and then working past the crew's prejudices.**" She uncovers her food and begins to dig in, not really paying attention to what it is that has been sent
As you look back, the grey creature has disappeared already.
**"It's hard to catch a break, isn't it. I just wonder when all our luck runs out."**

"**When the Force abandons us... Or we abandon the Force,**" she says between bites. "**Speaking of... We should have our own training sessions. There's much I'm sure we'll be able to teach other. Especially since there's the high probability of going against Reapers.**"
He pauses, **"I'm not... the sharing type."**

**"However, you're right... You deserve to know everything about the Reapers, given we're likely going to go up against them at one point or another... Each Reaper is different, specialising in their own form of warfare. Each is a Frankenstein project with the backing of a dozen Imperial scientists. They're dangerous, every one of them, for entirely different reasons. Some are exceptional Sorcerers, others unmatched melee combatants, often specialisting in their own personal fighting style, lightsaber type, and martial arts training."**
Lexi listens attentively. It made sense as clones they would be designed to specialize. "**And what was your specialization?**" she dares to ask.
**"As I said... I'm not the sharing type. Its better you don't know."**

"**Better for me? Or for you?**" She skips a beat before continuing. "**The way I see it, we're going to be working together as a team. Teams work best when you know your partner's strengths and weaknesses. If you don't want to 'share' now that's fine, but at some point I hope you'll stop thinking you have to keep your your past from me  and trust me.**"

**"Better for us both. You can't reveal any information if caught, and as you can imagine a Reaper's ability to keep their specialization secret is key to their catching the enemy off guard. Even at the cost of keeping secrets from their allies. You ask me to trust you, well... I ask you, instead, to trust me."**
"**Alright,**" she says, finishing her meal, half eaten and begins cleaning up her area. "**Then that will be the way of it.**"
**"Thank you, for understanding... This is becoming quite the adventure isn't it."**

"**Adventure almost seems too mild a word... Enterprise... Undertaking...**" She mulls over the words as not for the first time she dwells on the task set before her. "**For however long it lasts, I'm grateful to not be on this path alone.**"
**"I'll drink to that."** He takes a swig of the alcoholic beveage included in their meal, only available to Imperial Officers.
With that, the screen wipes black and we end Episode I.
